skill.name: incident-runbook-templates
skill.description: Create structured incident response runbooks with step-by-step procedures, escalation paths, and recovery actions. Use when building runbooks, responding to incidents, or establishing incident response processes.
skill.summary: Production-ready templates for incident response runbooks covering detection, triage, mitigation, resolution, and communication.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 62 static findings are false positives from Markdown documentation. The skill contains only template documentation with code examples (bash, kubectl, SQL) in fenced code blocks. No executable code, no prompt injection attempts, and no security risks detected. Safe to publish.

content.value_statement: Quickly build comprehensive incident response runbooks with proven templates. Reduce incident resolution time by providing step-by-step procedures for detection, triage, mitigation, and communication.
actual_capabilities: Generate structured runbooks for service outages with detection, triage, and mitigation steps
Create database incident procedures for connection issues, replication lag, and disk space emergencies
Build escalation matrices with contact information and trigger conditions
Provide communication templates for internal notifications, status updates, and resolution announcements
Include rollback procedures and verification steps for Kubernetes deployments and database migrations
Define severity levels (SEV1-SEV4) with response time guidelines and impact criteria
limitations: Templates are examples that must be customized for your specific infrastructure and services
Does not execute commands - users must manually run the provided kubectl, SQL, and bash commands
Requires familiarity with Kubernetes, PostgreSQL, and basic incident response concepts
Does not integrate with monitoring tools like PagerDuty, Grafana, or Sentry out of the box

use_cases.title: On-call engineer responding to SEV1 incident
use_cases.description: An on-call engineer receives a PagerDuty alert at 3 AM for a complete service outage. They use this skill to quickly access the service outage runbook template, which guides them through checking pod status, reviewing recent deployments, and executing a rollback if needed.

use_cases.title: SRE team building documentation library
use_cases.description: An SRE team is establishing their incident management process. They use this skill to generate standardized runbooks for different services (payment processing, database, API gateway) ensuring consistent response procedures across the organization.

use_cases.title: Startup establishing incident response process
use_cases.description: A growing startup needs to document their incident response procedures as they scale. They use this skill to create their first set of runbooks, define severity levels, and build communication templates for stakeholder updates during incidents.

prompt_templates.title: Generate service outage runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a runbook for a Redis cache service outage. Include steps for checking pod status, memory usage, and connection counts. Add rollback procedures.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to create a runbook for a Redis cache service in their Kubernetes environment.

prompt_templates.title: Build database incident runbook
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a database runbook for MySQL replication lag. Include queries to check lag, identify slow queries, and steps to promote a replica if needed.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a MySQL-specific database incident runbook with replication failure scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Create escalation matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an escalation matrix for a e-commerce platform. Include conditions for SEV1-SEV4 incidents with appropriate contacts for engineering, legal, finance, and executive teams.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to define escalation paths for a multi-team e-commerce organization.

prompt_templates.title: Generate communication templates
prompt_templates.prompt: Create customer-facing communication templates for a data privacy incident. Include initial notification, update, and resolution messages that satisfy legal requirements.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs communication templates for a sensitive incident requiring customer notification.

output_examples.input: Create a runbook for API gateway high latency
output_examples.output: Generated a comprehensive API Gateway Latency Runbook with detection steps (check latency p99, error rates, upstream service health), triage procedures (identify bottlenecks, check dependency latency), mitigation actions (enable caching, increase timeouts, scale upstream services), and verification steps to confirm latency has returned to baseline.

output_examples.input: Build a database connection pool exhaustion runbook for PostgreSQL
output_examples.output: Generated a PostgreSQL Connection Pool Runbook with SQL queries to identify long-running connections, steps to terminate idle connections, configuration tuning recommendations (max_connections, pool size), and prevention strategies including connection pooling best practices and monitoring alerts.

output_examples.input: Create communication templates for a payment processing outage
output_examples.output: Generated three communication templates: (1) Initial internal notification with severity classification, impact assessment, and incident commander assignment, (2) Status update template with mitigation progress and ETA, (3) Customer-facing message with transparent impact description, estimated resolution time, and apology with compensation offer if applicable.

best_practices: Customize templates with your actual service names, Slack channels, PagerDuty schedules, and dashboard URLs before using in production
Test runbook procedures during game days or chaos engineering exercises to validate accuracy and completeness
Update runbooks after every incident based on lessons learned and new insights from postmortems
Include verification steps after each mitigation action to confirm the fix worked before moving to the next step
Link to actual dashboards (Grafana, Sentry) and runbooks in your incident response tools for quick access during emergencies
anti_patterns: Do not copy templates without customizing placeholders (service names, commands, contacts) to match your environment
Do not skip the verification steps - always confirm a mitigation action worked before proceeding
Do not work in isolation during incidents - use the escalation matrix to involve appropriate teams early
Do not treat runbooks as static documents - review and update them quarterly or after major infrastructure changes
Do not assume context is preserved during incidents - write steps clearly enough for a sleep-deprived engineer at 3 AM

faq.question: Can I modify these templates for my specific infrastructure?
faq.answer: Yes, these templates are designed to be customized. Replace placeholder service names, commands, URLs, and contact information with your actual infrastructure details.

faq.question: Do these runbooks work with any cloud provider?
faq.answer: Yes, the templates are cloud-agnostic but primarily show Kubernetes examples. Adapt commands for AWS, GCP, or Azure specific tools as needed.

faq.question: How often should I update my runbooks?
faq.answer: Update runbooks after every incident to capture lessons learned, and review all runbooks quarterly to ensure they reflect your current infrastructure.

faq.question: Can I use these for non-production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes, adapt the severity levels and response times for your environment. For staging, consider using SEV3-SEV4 classifications and longer response times.

faq.question: Do I need to be a Kubernetes expert to use these templates?
faq.answer: Basic Kubernetes knowledge is helpful for the service outage templates, but the concepts apply to any infrastructure. Adapt the commands for your deployment platform.

faq.question: How do I integrate these with my monitoring tools?
faq.answer: Replace the example dashboard URLs (Grafana, Sentry) and alert examples with your actual monitoring tool URLs and alert configurations.
